The Market
03
The Market

More jobs than ever.
Harder than ever to land one.

Global Capability Centers are hiring across India at record numbers. At the same time, the old entry-level path is closing. Both are happening right now, and the engineers who see why will be the ones who get in.

THE BOOM
2,100+
GCCs now operating in India
~2.4M
people employed across them
~1M
AI-related roles projected in India by 2026
THE SQUEEZE
20-25%
drop in traditional entry-level tech hiring
~120K
trained AI professionals available today
~50%
of graduates rated job-ready on skills
THE GAP, IN ONE VIEW · AI ROLES vs TRAINED ENGINEERS BY 2026
AI roles projected~1,000,000
Trained AI professionals available~120,000
Fewer than one qualified engineer exists for every two roles. That shortfall is the opportunity.

The demand is here. The trained people are not. That gap is roughly one qualified AI engineer for every two open roles. This program exists to put you on the short side of it.

INDUSTRY OUTLOOK
GCC hiring in India is projected to keep expanding through 2030, with AI and data roles leading demand. The scarce input is not jobs. It is engineers who can prove they can build.
Sources: NASSCOM & Zinnov GCC data 2025-26 · EY GCC Pulse · India Skills Report 2026 · industry AI-talent estimates
×
The Problem
04
The Real Wall

The problem was never
learning the skills.

Plenty of engineers can learn AI. The ones who stall usually skipped the foundation to get there. They build impressive agents, then lose the offer at the coding round. Or they have both, and no one who hires ever sees them.

THREE GATES BETWEEN YOU AND THE OFFER
1
The coding round
A strong AI portfolio still gets cut for the medium DSA problems no one drilled properly.
2
The proof gap
"Familiar with AI" reads like every other resume. A system you built and can explain reads like a hire.
3
The visibility problem
A good profile still has to reach the right desk. Most strong roles move through people, not application forms.
WHERE CANDIDATES FALL AWAY
All applicants
↓
Pass the resume
↓
Clear coding
↓
Offer
Each gate removes people. This program is built to keep you moving through all three.

"The coding screen hasn't gone anywhere. What decides the offer now is what you've actually built and whether you can defend it. That part is learnable, and it's what most people skip."

This program is designed to clear all three gates in one 14-week sequence.
RECRUITER PERSPECTIVE
Hiring teams increasingly ask candidates to walk through a system they built. A portfolio you can defend answers the question a resume cannot.
×
Why AI
05
Why AI, Why Now

The job changed while
you were job hunting.

AI has moved past chat. It now reasons, calls tools, pulls in real data, and runs as coordinated agents inside production systems. Calling an LLM is easy. Designing an agentic system that holds up, measuring it, and explaining your choices is not. That second skill is what interviewers have started to test.

GETS YOU AN INTERVIEW
·Calls an LLM API
·Uses ChatGPT to write code
·Lists "familiar with AI" on a resume
·Builds features
·Learns a framework
GETS YOU THE OFFER
✓Designs reliable multi-agent systems
✓Grounds AI in real data with RAG
✓Ships a system they can defend under questioning
✓Evaluates cost, latency and failure modes
✓Explains architectural trade-offs
71%
of leaders would hire someone with AI skills over someone more senior without them
300%+
growth in demand for GenAI skills since 2024
30-60%
pay premium for engineers who can build with AI

Prompt tricks age fast. Architecture, judgment, evaluation, and knowing why you made a call do not. Those are the skills AI makes more valuable, not less. DSA gets you through the screen. Agentic AI keeps you worth hiring after it.

KEY TAKEAWAY
The market is not asking whether you have heard of AI. It is asking whether you can build with it and defend what you built.
Sources: Microsoft & LinkedIn Work Trend Index · industry GenAI compensation surveys 2025-26
×
Who It's For
06
Built for Early-Career Engineers

You will recognise
yourself here.

For engineers with 0 to 4 years of experience: working, between roles, or about to start out.

💻
Early-Career Software Engineers

A few years in, and you want your fundamentals sharp for interviews and AI skills that actually set your profile apart.

⚙
Fullstack, Backend & Frontend Engineers

You have shipped features. Now you want to design RAG, multi-agent workflows, and real deployment into the apps you build.

🧠
Software Engineers Moving Into AI

You come from a software background and want into AI-native work, with projects to show and the design chops to hold an interview.

🎓
Recent Graduates & Career Switchers

Chasing a first or next engineering role, and you want interview prep, a real project, and job-search coaching in one place.

💡
DID YOU KNOW
Roughly half of India's engineering graduates are rated job-ready on skills. The gap is rarely intelligence. It is structured practice and proof of what you can build, which is exactly what this program provides.
Prerequisite:comfort with one OOP language (Python, C++, Java or JavaScript). No AI experience required. A self-paced Python module is there if you want it.
WHO IT IS NOT FOR
Engineers with more than five years of experience, or anyone looking for a passive, watch-only course. This is live and hands-on.

The Difference
08
Why This Program

Most programs teach.
This one gets you hired.

01
Every track points at the offer

DSA handles the screen. Agentic AI gives you something to talk about. Career coaching closes it. Nothing in the syllabus is there by accident.

02
Live, and built around what you make

Every core session runs live. The Agentic AI track is a run of builds that get harder each week, from a single RAG agent to a full multi-agent system.

03
Ordered on purpose

Fundamentals come first because that is what the screen tests. Agentic AI comes next so your skills last past this hiring cycle. Coaching comes last, when you have something to sell.

04
A project worth showing

You leave with Finnie or SmartDesk AI: a production-style build that shows you can think through a whole system, not just wire up a demo.

HOW IT COMPARES
A TYPICAL AI COURSE
• Recorded videos, watch alone
• Toy notebooks, no real system
• No interview or coding prep
• You are on your own after
THIS PROGRAM
✓ Live sessions with practitioners
✓ A capstone you can defend
✓ DSA plus mocks plus coaching
✓ Recruiter Connect at the end
WHY THIS MATTERS
A certificate says you attended. A working system, interview reps, and a recruiter introduction say you are ready. Employers respond to the second.
×
The Network
09
07 · Why Talent500

Being ready is half of it.
Being seen is the rest.

We run this with Talent500, which places engineers into roles at GCCs and multinationals across India. It is the difference between sending applications into a void and having someone put your name forward.

01
Curated, not a job board

Your profile gets matched to specific openings, not dropped onto a pile of two thousand applications.

02
Direct GCC & Fortune 500 access

A hiring pipeline spanning Global Capability Centers, MNCs, product companies and Fortune 500 organisations.

03
One profile, many doors

Showcase your skills once, then get matched to curated roles across tech, product, data, analytics and more.

04
Proof, not keywords

Assessments back up what your resume claims. Recruiters trust that more than a page of the right buzzwords.

The best roles rarely reach a public job board. They move through networks. Talent500 is how you get into that flow.

×
DSA
11
TRACK 1 · CLEARS THE CODING ROUND

Data Structures & Algorithms

Why it matters: the coding screen still stands between you and almost every offer. Six weeks in pseudocode, so what you learn works in any language. It opens with a week on using AI to prep faster.

01
AI-Powered Interview Prep
Claude Code for interviews, prompt engineering for problem-solving, AI-assisted debugging
02
Sorting Algorithms
Merge/Quick/Counting/Radix, divide-and-conquer, complexity analysis, choosing the right sort
03
Recursion & Backtracking
Call stack, base vs recursive case, N-Queens/Sudoku/permutations, memoization
04
Trees
BSTs, traversals, balanced trees, heaps & priority queues, tries
05
Graphs
Adjacency list/matrix, BFS/DFS, Dijkstra & Bellman-Ford, topological sort, cycles
06
Dynamic Programming
Overlapping subproblems, memoization vs tabulation, Knapsack/LCS/LIS, 2D DP
HOW THE SIX WEEKS BUILD
Wk 1
Wk 2
Wk 3
Wk 4
Wk 5
Wk 6
OUTCOME
Read complexity, pick the right approach under pressure, and walk into a coding round without dread.
TOOLS
Claude CodePseudocode
×
Agentic AI
12
TRACK 2 · BUILDS THE PROOF

Agentic AI Engineering

This is the track that sets your profile apart. Anyone can call an LLM. Few can design an agentic system that works, measure it, and stand behind it. Over five weeks you take these systems apart, piece by piece, and build one of your own.

THE SIX BUILDING BLOCKS
1
Agents. Architectures, design patterns, when agentic beats deterministic
2
Retrieval. RAG pipelines grounded in real documents, not hallucination
3
Orchestration. Multi-agent workflows, state, tool use, function calling
4
Observability. Tracing, evaluation, cost and latency optimisation
5
Deployment. MCP, Streamlit, Hugging Face, cloud
THE WORKFLOW YOU'LL BUILD
User problem
Prompt design
LLM reasoning
RAG retrieval
Agent orchestration
Deploy (MCP)
Observe & evaluate

Every week stacks on the one before, from a single RAG agent to a full multi-agent system you can watch and measure. The next three pages show exactly what you build.

×
RAG & Agents
13
WEEK 7

Ground AI in real knowledge.

OBJECTIVE

Build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation agent that answers from real documents instead of making things up.

WHY IT MATTERS

A RAG agent that cites its sources is the first thing on your profile that says builder rather than someone who has used ChatGPT.

HANDS-ON

Build a working Q&A agent grounded in a real document set, wired up with embeddings and a vector store.

TOPICS COVERED
›What AI agents are: architectures & design patterns
›Introduction to the LangChain framework
›Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline
›Vector databases: ChromaDB and Pinecone
›Building a RAG-based Q&A agent
LangChainChromaDBPineconeOpenAI / Claude API
HOW IT FITS TOGETHER
Question
User asks
→
Embed
Vectorise query
→
Retrieve
Vector DB
→
Augment
Add context
→
LLM
Grounded answer
→
Cite
With sources
LEARNING OUTCOME

A data-grounded Q&A agent that answers from what it retrieves, not what it guesses.

HOW RECRUITERS VALUE IT

RAG shows up in more GCC job posts than almost any other applied-AI skill. A working pipeline is proof you can point to.

×
Multi-Agent Systems
14
WEEK 8

Orchestrate agents that coordinate.

OBJECTIVE

Go from one agent to several working together in LangGraph, with shared state, tool use, and function calling.

WHY IT MATTERS

One agent is a demo. Several agents coordinating is what real products run on, and it is what system-design rounds have started asking about.

HANDS-ON

Build a pipeline where specialised agents hand off state and call tools to finish a task start to finish.

TOPICS COVERED
›Introduction to LangGraph for orchestration
›Designing multi-agent workflows
›Agent communication & state management
›Tool-use agents and function calling
›Building a multi-agent pipeline
LangGraphLangChainPython
HOW IT FITS TOGETHER
Planner
Breaks down task
→
Agent A
Specialist
→
Agent B
Specialist
→
Shared state
Passed along
→
Tools
Function calls
→
Result
Task complete
LEARNING OUTCOME

Run multi-agent workflows and explain, out loud, why you built them the way you did.

HOW RECRUITERS VALUE IT

Orchestration reads as senior thinking, early. It is the gap between using an LLM and designing a system.

×
Observability & Deploy
15
WEEKS 9-10

Ship systems you can trust.

OBJECTIVE

Trace, test, and monitor your agents in LangSmith, then put real applications live with MCP, Streamlit, and Hugging Face.

WHY IT MATTERS

Building something that works once is easy. Measuring its cost, speed, and accuracy, then shipping it, is what gets you hired and kept.

HANDS-ON

Wire an agent up to LangSmith, run evaluation loops, then ship an MCP-compatible app to a live URL.

TOPICS COVERED
›Why observability matters for agents
›LangSmith for tracing & debugging
›Evaluating agent performance & accuracy
›Cost tracking & latency optimisation
›Model Context Protocol (MCP) & deployment
›Deploy on Streamlit & Hugging Face Spaces
LangSmithMCP SDKStreamlitHugging Face
HOW IT FITS TOGETHER
Build
Agent app
→
Trace
LangSmith
→
Evaluate
Accuracy, cost
→
Optimise
Latency, spend
→
Deploy
MCP, Streamlit
→
Monitor
In production
LEARNING OUTCOME

Trace, test, tune, and deploy an agentic app across its full production lifecycle.

HOW RECRUITERS VALUE IT

Few early-career candidates can do evaluation and production work. It moves you from promising to ready.

Finnie

6-agent systemLangGraphRAG
SmartDesk AI

TRACK 3 · CONVERTS TO OFFERS

Career Coaching

Why it matters: skills get you shortlisted. Knowing how to close gets you the offer. Three weeks turning what you have built into a resume, interview reps, and a stronger number at the end.

Resume Building & Profile
ATS-friendly technical resume, LinkedIn & GitHub optimisation, portfolio curation for 0-4 YOE, personal branding

The Format
19
How You Learn

Live where it counts.
Self-paced where it helps.

The core sessions are live and hands-on. Foundations and revision are self-paced, so you catch up on the basics and go back over anything on your own time.

● LIVE (WEEKS 1-14)
●All core sessions (Weeks 1-14) delivered live & interactive
●Progressively harder builds, from a single RAG agent to a full multi-agent system
●Code review & architecture feedback on your capstone
●Mock interviews with peer and coach feedback
◯ SELF-PACED
●Optional self-paced Python fundamentals (Week 0)
●Implement DSA in any language you choose
●Independent capstone project work
●Recorded content to revisit anytime
WHAT A TYPICAL WEEK LOOKS LIKE
Live class
Learn the concept, see it built
→
Build
Apply it to a real problem
→
Review
Feedback on your code
→
Iterate
Sharpen before the next week
LEARNING OUTCOME
You do not just watch. You build every week, get your work reviewed, and walk away with something real by the end.

ELIGIBILITY. Recruiter Connect, provided by Talent500, is available to learners with 0 to less than 5 years of experience. It currently applies to these IK tracks: Backend, Full Stack, Frontend, Test, iOS, Android, Early Engineering, Machine Learning, Data Engineering, Data Science, Embedded Systems, Site Reliability Engineering and Cyber Security. It does not currently apply to Engineering Manager, Technical Program Manager, Product Manager (Tech), Data Analyst & Business Analyst, or AWS Cloud Solutions Architect. This program is an eligible track.

Answers
26
Questions, Answered

Questions, answered.

What language are the DSA sessions taught in?

All DSA sessions are language-agnostic, taught entirely in pseudocode. You implement in any language you choose during practice.

Do I need to know Python before joining?

Python is a prerequisite for the Agentic AI module. Self-paced Python fundamentals are provided if you need them; live sessions may run based on cohort demand.

What frameworks does the Agentic AI module use?

Primarily LangChain (agents and RAG pipelines) and LangGraph (orchestrating multi-agent systems and managing state).

Which vector databases are covered?

ChromaDB and Pinecone. Others may be introduced depending on project and industry relevance.

What's used for observability and evaluation?

LangSmith, integrated with LangGraph, for tracing, debugging, monitoring, and evaluating agent performance and cost.

Where are the Agentic AI projects deployed?

Streamlit, Hugging Face Spaces, or cloud platforms like AWS and GCP, depending on project scope.

What is Model Context Protocol (MCP)?

A standardized protocol for how AI models interact with external tools and data sources. Covered in Week 10 alongside deployment.

Is the program live or self-paced?

All core sessions (Weeks 1-14) are live. The Week 11 capstone includes both live sessions and independent project work.

What does it cost?

₹70,000 (from ₹1,00,000), with 0%% no-cost EMI from ₹5,834/month. A 14-day refund window applies from your first class.

Who is this program for?

Software engineers with 0 to 4 years of experience: fullstack, backend and frontend developers, people moving into AI roles, recent graduates, and career switchers.

×
